author: hsliuustc0106
body: This is validated only on Qwen-Image/H200, but it becomes the default for every CUDA diffusion pipeline. Can we scope the override through `_DIFFUSION_IR_OP_PRIORITY_FUNCS` for the Qwen-Image family, or add representative non-Qwen A/B results to justify the global change?

author: hsliuustc0106
body: Please make the requested regression test cover compiled/non-compiled defaults and `VLLM_USE_OINK_OPS` for both norm ops. Since this switches kernel providers, also report `test_qwen_image_2512_matches_diffusers_pixelwise` (or an equivalent same-seed main/PR accuracy comparison) alongside the latency numbers.

author: hsliuustc0106
body: This restores `["vllm_c", "native"]`, but the PR does not add a regression test for the inductor-active case that broke in #4964. Existing tests do not cover `CudaOmniPlatform.get_default_ir_op_priority`, so a future upstream sync could silently reintroduce `["native"]` and only fail later in nightly perf. Please add a small CPU test asserting that inductor mode still prefers `vllm_c`, together with the `VLLM_USE_OINK_OPS` behavior.

author: Gaohan123
body: The pytest mark is not complete

author: NumberWan
body:
The changes will affecting all the diffusion model which using  ** the same `vllm.model_executor.layers.layernorm.RMSNorm` IR path** 

The affected models should be on the list below:
FLUX.1-dev / FLUX.2 / FLUX.2 Klein, Wan22S2V, HunyuanVideo15, SD3, OmniGen2, LongCat, Ernie, Ovis, LTX-2 / LTX-2.3 .

FLUX.1-dev has been tested to justify global changes
After fixed: Serving perf (512², 8 steps, n=5) 
latency_mean -14.1%
The details have been added in the description.
